name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 18.08.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 18.08.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 15 August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1039.81 lakh ha as compared to 1002.41 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 94.8 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 15 August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1039.81 lakh ha as compared to 1002.41 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 94.8 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal in many parts of Central India, Gujarat, Rajasthan, East Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and NE India.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal in many parts of Central India, Gujarat, Rajasthan, East Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and NE India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01 -Aug 17 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)18%; (ii) Northwest India: 13%; (iii) Central India: 4%; and South Peninsula: 6%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01 -Aug 17 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)18%; (ii) Northwest India: 13%; (iii) Central India: 4%; and South Peninsula: 6%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fresh  low  pressure  area  over  west  central  adjoining  northwest  Bay  of  Bengal,  off  north Andhra Pradesh and south Odisha coasts and its likely intensification into a depression during next 24 Hours.
texts.text: Fresh  low  pressure  area  over  west  central  adjoining  northwest  Bay  of  Bengal,  off  north Andhra Pradesh and south Odisha coasts and its likely intensification into a depression during next 24 Hours.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy to very heavy rainfall over Goa, Maharashtra (including  Mumbai),  Gujarat State during next 7 days with Extremely heavy falls over Konkan (including Mumbai) & Goa, Ghat area of Madhya Maharashtra during 17 th  -19 th , Gujarat Region and during 18 th -20 th ; Saurashtra on 19 th  & 20 th  August with peak activity on 19 th  & 20 th  August.
texts.text: Heavy to very heavy rainfall over Goa, Maharashtra (including  Mumbai),  Gujarat State during next 7 days with Extremely heavy falls over Konkan (including Mumbai) & Goa, Ghat area of Madhya Maharashtra during 17 th  -19 th , Gujarat Region and during 18 th -20 th ; Saurashtra on 19 th  & 20 th  August with peak activity on 19 th  & 20 th  August.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy to very heavy rainfall  likely  over  Andhra  Pradesh,  Telangana,  Chhattisgarh  and Odisha during next two days with isolated extremely heavy over coastal Andhra Pradesh during 17 th  - 18 th , Odisha and Telangana on 18th August.
texts.text: Heavy to very heavy rainfall  likely  over  Andhra  Pradesh,  Telangana,  Chhattisgarh  and Odisha during next two days with isolated extremely heavy over coastal Andhra Pradesh during 17 th  - 18 th , Odisha and Telangana on 18th August.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over the most parts of the country.
texts.text: Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over the most parts of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · A  low  pressure  area  is  likely  to  form  over  northwest  Bay  of  Bengal  &  Adjoining Bangladesh region around 24 th  August, 2025.
texts.text: A  low  pressure  area  is  likely  to  form  over  northwest  Bay  of  Bengal  &  Adjoining Bangladesh region around 24 th  August, 2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 7 th  August 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 135.28 billion cubic meter, which is 74.1% of total live storage capacity (182.496 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 107 percent of last year's storage and 124 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 7 th  August 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 135.28 billion cubic meter, which is 74.1% of total live storage capacity (182.496 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 107 percent of last year's storage and 124 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases, at some fields, has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Rice (Varieties: PR 114, PR 126 & PR 131) (Southern Rice  Black  Streaked  Dwarf  Virus  (SRBSDV))  in  Haryana  and  (Swarming  Caterpillar)  in Assam.  The  Advisory  has  been  issued  by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall  intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases, at some fields, has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Rice (Varieties: PR 114, PR 126 & PR 131) (Southern Rice  Black  Streaked  Dwarf  Virus  (SRBSDV))  in  Haryana  and  (Swarming  Caterpillar)  in Assam.  The  Advisory  has  been  issued  by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall  intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (07 Aug - 13 Aug 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 25%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 June to 13 Aug 2025) shows no departure from LPA.
texts.text: During the week (07 Aug - 13 Aug 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 25%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 June to 13 Aug 2025) shows no departure from LPA.

texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The  monsoon  trough  at  mean  sea  level  now  passes  through  Bhuj,  Ahmedabad,  Akola, Ramgundam,  center  of  well  marked  Low  pressure  area  over  west  central  and  adjoining northwest Bay of Bengal & north Andhra Pradesh-south Odisha coasts.
texts.text: The  monsoon  trough  at  mean  sea  level  now  passes  through  Bhuj,  Ahmedabad,  Akola, Ramgundam,  center  of  well  marked  Low  pressure  area  over  west  central  and  adjoining northwest Bay of Bengal & north Andhra Pradesh-south Odisha coasts.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rainfall is likely to be above normal over most part of Central India, West India, Odisha, Jharkhand,  and  many  parts  of  NW  India,  Telangana,  and  Gangetic  West  Bengal.  Below Normal over south Uttar Pradesh and many parts of NE India, Kerala, Rayalaseema and coastal Andhra Pradesh.
texts.text: Rainfall is likely to be above normal over most part of Central India, West India, Odisha, Jharkhand,  and  many  parts  of  NW  India,  Telangana,  and  Gangetic  West  Bengal.  Below Normal over south Uttar Pradesh and many parts of NE India, Kerala, Rayalaseema and coastal Andhra Pradesh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 14 August 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major parts of M.P, central parts of Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Kerala,  Karnataka,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  Rayalseema region of A.P., Tamil Nadu and southern parts of Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 14 August 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major parts of M.P, central parts of Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Kerala,  Karnataka,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  Rayalseema region of A.P., Tamil Nadu and southern parts of Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  9  years  in  Northern  parts  of Bihar, in  parts  of  Assam, north  eastern  parts  of  U.P.,  Assam,  Gujarat,  in  western  and  southern  parts  of  Rajasthan, southern parts of Odisha, few western districts of MP and coastal districts of AP.
texts.text: It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  9  years  in  Northern  parts  of Bihar, in  parts  of  Assam, north  eastern  parts  of  U.P.,  Assam,  Gujarat,  in  western  and  southern  parts  of  Rajasthan, southern parts of Odisha, few western districts of MP and coastal districts of AP.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rice : About 398.59 lakh ha (98.9% of Normal) area coverage under Kharif Rice as compared to 362.92 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rice : About 398.59 lakh ha (98.9% of Normal) area coverage under Kharif Rice as compared to 362.92 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
